---
title: "Creating A New Column Configuration"
---

---
title: "Creating a New Column Configuration"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-for-Salesforce/lby1756675232616"
section: "Hyland for Salesforce"
---

This section explains how to configure the column names and columns that are displayed in the Document List window. The Document List window displays a list of supported files captured using Hyland for Salesforce and added as documents.
To create a new column configuration:

1.  Open a web browser and log on to the Salesforce environment with your credentials. The Home page is displayed.
2.  From the top-right of the Home page, click the App Launcher button.   ![App Launcher button](data:image/gif;base64,)   The App Launcher drop-down menu is displayed.
3.  In the App Launcher search box, enter Solution Configuration. The search results are displayed.
4.  Click Solution Configurations from the search results. The Solution Configurations login page is displayed.
5.  Log on to the Hyland for Salesforce application if you aren't authenticated already. The Solution Configurations page is displayed.
6.  From the left hand-pane, under Mappings, select an object and then select the Column Configuration tab.
7.  From the Select Content Type drop-down list, select a Content Type. The selected Content Type is associated with the Salesforce object.
8.  Under the Available Columns list, select one or more column names and then select >.  Tip: Press Ctrl to select multiple column names.   Note: The Available Columns list is updated based on the Content Type selected.  The selected column names are displayed under the Displayed Columns list.
For example, if you choose to display Address and Purchase Date in the Document List window, only these columns are displayed. All captured files shown in Document List window show the values associated with these properties.

9.  From the top-right, click Update. A confirmation message is displayed. The columns and column names for a Content Type and dislayed in the Document List window are saved.
